notes: [cl-crosshair.core.master_gating | logic] Master gating + enable/size/alpha early-outs  -  Port gates only on Visible + crosshair_enabled/size/alpha/number; Base's full suppress set (scoreboard/camera/intermission2/game-stopped/lockview/spectatee==-1/HEALTH<=0/DrawCrosshair mutator hook/viewloc/minigame menu/CAMERA_SPECTATOR==2/hud!=HUD_NORMAL) is reduced to the NetGame Visible toggle (havePlayer at NetGame:3366).

[cl-crosshair.core.origin_chase | logic,values,presentation] Crosshair origin + third-person chase trace & body-alpha fade  -  Crosshair is always drawn at the geometric panel centre (DrawPanel: center = Size2*0.5f). The chase-camera branch (trace from player org along view_forward MOVE_WORLDONLY, project the impact, fade the player body alpha toward crosshair_chase_playeralpha while it obstructs the view, restore on chase-off via the crosshair_chase_state latch) is not implemented.; crosshair_chase / crosshair_chase_playeralpha / crosshair_2d are now REGISTERED in RegisterDefaults (CrosshairPanel.cs:323-325) with the Base default values, but have NO reader — they are inert settable cvars (the chase trace + body-alpha fade + side-scroller selection are still unported).

[cl-crosshair.trueaim.showimpact | logic,presentation] Debug: draw crosshair at the reprojected impact point (crosshair_hittest_showimpact)  -  Base (crosshair.qc:306-321): the hit-test block reprojects the box-trace endpoint into screen space (wcross_origin) and, unless crosshair_hittest_showimpact is set, restores wcross_origin to the geometric centre. With showimpact=1 the crosshair is left drawn AT the projected impact point (a debug/aim-assist cue). The port always draws at the geometric panel centre (DrawPanel: center = Size2*0.5f) and never reprojects — the cvar is registered with the Base default (0) but has no reader.; Tied to the same missing screen-space reprojection as the trueaim HITOBSTRUCTION test; default 0 so the visible default behaviour matches Base.

[cl-crosshair.color.special_modes | logic,presentation] Crosshair color: weapon / health+armor / fixed (crosshair_color_special)  -  crosshair_color_special 3 (rainbow: randomvec()*brightness re-rolled every rainbow_delay, crosshair.qc:189-196) is not ported — ColorModeCvar (CrosshairPanel.cs:537-542) maps only 0/1/2, so mode 3 silently falls back to fixed color. The rainbow_brightness/delay cvars are registered but dead.

[cl-crosshair.anim.pickup_pulse | liveness] Item-pickup crosshair pulse (crosshair_pickup)  -  FIXED (host path): UpdatePickupFeed now calls CrosshairPanel.PulsePickup() (NetGame:2864) when a new weapon or a resource jump is detected on the LocalServerPlayer (re-baselined on respawn so the spawn loadout isn't counted). The crosshair bumps on item pickup on the host/listen-server. Still host-only: the detection reads LocalServerPlayer, so a pure remote client (LocalServerPlayer==null) gets no pulse — the networked STAT(LAST_PICKUP) equivalent is not read back into the panel client-side.; Pickup trigger is derived (new-weapon-owned or per-frame resource jump > threshold) rather than the Base STAT(LAST_PICKUP) tick; approximate but matches the visible cue for the host player.

[cl-crosshair.anim.hit_indication | liveness] Hit-indication scale bump + color flash (crosshair_hitindication)  -  Host-only: HitFlash=1 is set only inside `if (_server is not null)` (NetGame:2532, driven by the host-side damagetext mutator drain). A pure remote client gets no hit-indication bump/flash even though HitDamageDealtTotal is networked (ServerNet.cs) and the client diffs it for the hit SOUND — but not for the crosshair flash.; Coupled to the damagetext mutator events rather than the Base path (UpdateDamage diffs STAT(HITSOUND_DAMAGE_DEALT_TOTAL) on STAT(HIT_TIME) advance); fires on local-player-dealt damage only (ev.Attacker==localPlayer && ev.Target!=localPlayer).

[cl-crosshair.anim.smooth_ease | presentation] Goal-based smooth scale/alpha/color ease (crosshair_effect_time)  -  Color is NOT eased: Base eases scale, alpha AND color toward goal over effect_time (crosshair.qc:444 `wcross_color = f*wcross_color + (1-f)*wcross_color_prev`); the port's SmoothGoal eases only scale+alpha and sets color directly each frame. Visually equivalent only for slow color changes within the effect_time window.

[cl-crosshair.anim.switch_crossfade | liveness] Weapon-switch crosshair image cross-fade  -  Keyed on the resolved Texture2D changing rather than the wcross_name/resolution string pair; with the per-weapon dict unpopulated the trigger only fires on a `crosshair` cvar change, NOT on every weapon switch — so in normal play (one crosshair number for all weapons) the cross-fade effectively never runs.

[cl-crosshair.trueaim.teammate_blur_signal | presentation] Teammate shrink + wall/teammate blur signalling  -  Blur is a 5x5 spread of 0.04-alpha texture copies (matches Base's CROSSHAIR_DO_BLUR for textured crosshairs, crosshair.qc:585-596); only applies to the textured path (DrawCrosshairTexture), not the vector fallback (DrawVectorCrosshair).

[cl-crosshair.ring.vortex_charge | liveness] Vortex / Overkill-Nex charge ring (+ inner chargepool / moving-average ring)  -  FIXED (host path): UpdateCrosshairWeaponRings now feeds CrosshairPanel.ChargeFraction = st.VortexCharge and .ChargePool = st.VortexChargePoolAmmo (NetGame:3153-54) while a vortex/vaporizer/*nex* weapon is active off the host-side slot-0 WeaponSlotState (the C# home of QC wepent.*). The Vortex charge ring + inner chargepool/moving-average ring now render for the host player. VortexCharge is genuinely produced server-side (Vortex.cs/OkNex.cs). Still host-only: read off LocalServerPlayer inside `if (_server is not null)`; a pure remote client (LocalServerPlayer==null) gets the -1 sentinel and no ring.; Minor fidelity: ChargePool is fed unconditionally for vortex-like weapons (default VortexChargePoolAmmo=1f), so the inner chargepool ring shows even when secondary cha

[cl-crosshair.ring.reload_ammo | liveness] Reload / ammo (clip) ring (+ Rifle ring art)  -  FIXED (host path): UpdateCrosshairWeaponRings now feeds CrosshairPanel.ClipLoad = st.ClipLoad and .ClipSize = st.ClipSize when the active slot has a clip (NetGame:3165-66). The ammo ring renders for every reloadable weapon on the host. ClipLoad/ClipSize are genuinely maintained on the slot by WeaponFireGate/WeaponFireDriver. Still host-only: read off LocalServerPlayer inside `if (_server is not null)`; remote clients get the -1/0 sentinel and no ring.

[cl-crosshair.ring.minelayer_hagar_arc | liveness] Mine Layer / Hagar load / Arc heat rings  -  FIXED (host path): UpdateCrosshairWeaponRings now feeds CrosshairPanel.HagarLoad (st.HagarLoad, NetGame:3177), .MineCount (live g_mines scan of mines owned by the player, 3189-92), and .ArcHeat (st.BeamHeat/OverheatMax with the overheat-jam latch, 3208-11) when the matching weapon is active. Mine-count, Hagar burst-load, and Arc overheat rings now render for the host player. HagarLoad/BeamHeat/ArcOverheat are genuinely produced server-side (Hagar.cs/Arc.cs). Still host-only: read off LocalServerPlayer inside `if (_server is not null)`; remote clients get the -1 sentinel and no ring.

[cl-crosshair.ring.switch_fade | liveness] Weapon-switch ring fade-out/in (wcross_ring_prev)  -  Now reachable on the host path: with the weapon-stat rings fed (UpdateCrosshairWeaponRings), DrawStatRing produces rings and calls SwitchRingFade (DrawStatRing:943), so the switch fade runs when the host player swaps between ring-bearing weapons. Still dead for a pure remote client (no ring ever draws there).

[cl-crosshair.objring.nade_capture_revive | presentation,liveness] Objective rings: NADE_TIMER > CAPTURE_PROGRESS > REVIVE_PROGRESS  -  PARTIAL FIX: REVIVE_PROGRESS is now fed (NetGame:2559, _fullHud.Crosshair.ReviveProgress = LocalServerPlayer?.ReviveProgress) alongside NadeTimer (2558), both host-only inside `if (_server is not null)`. Player.ReviveProgress is genuinely mirrored each tick by FreezeTag (FreezeTag.cs), so the host player's thaw ring now renders. STILL OPEN: CAPTURE_PROGRESS is never assigned on any production path (grep finds only test-side .CaptureProgress= plus the networked NetEntity.CaptureProgress field which ServerNet.cs explicitly leaves 0 with a comment that no gametype sets it) so the capture ring is DEAD; and although NetEntityState.ReviveProgress IS networked to remote clients (ServerNet.cs), NOTHING reads it back into the panel client-side — a pure remote cli

[cl-crosshair.per_weapon_2d_vehicle | logic,values,presentation,liveness] Per-weapon crosshair pic + 2D side-scroller + vehicle crosshair  -  Per-weapon crosshair: PerWeaponNumber dict is never populated by any caller (grep finds zero assignments outside the property def CrosshairPanel.cs:97), so every weapon shows the same `crosshair` number (Base maps each weapon to its own w_crosshair pic + w_crosshair_size multiplier).; crosshair_2d (side-scroller, default 54) is now REGISTERED (CrosshairPanel.cs:323) but has no reader — the FREEAIM/viewloc side-scroller crosshair selection is unported. HUD_Crosshair_Vehicle (info.vr_crosshair) has no port.

[raptor.spawn.setup | presentation] Spawn / setup (hitbox, resources, capability flags, sub-entities)  -  Cosmetic sub-entities not created: no raptor_spinner rotor entities (engine_left/right), no raptor_rotor_anglefix, no bomb1/bomb2/tur_head cosmetic models (gun1/gun2 ARE created for aim).; mass=1 is set on the entity but has NO behavioral effect — the port's MoveTypePhysics solver never reads .Mass, so the QC bounce-missile mass tuning is inert (value set for fidelity only).; FIXED (Wave-2 fixup): Spawn() no longer nulls vehicle.Touch, so the VehicleCommon.Touch dispatcher SpawnVehicle installed stands (crush + vr_impact path now live, see raptor.impact). Matches QC vr_spawn (does NOT clear .touch) and Racer (Racer.cs:189-190).

[raptor.enter | presentation] Board / enter (vr_enter)  -  tur_head.exteriormodeltoclient owner-binding and CSQCVehicleSetup (client viewport/HUD) not done here (cross-boundary client/net).; Carried-flag reparent ('-20 0 96') not modeled (CTF-on-raptor edge): a flag carried while boarding the raptor will not be repositioned to the cockpit mount.

[raptor.takeoff | presentation] Vertical takeoff sequence (raptor_takeoff)  -  Animation frame ramp tracked (VehAnimFrame) but drives no visible model; rotor avelocity ramp (90 + frame/25*25000) absent (no rotor entities) — presentation only.; Wave-2 added a button-clear (ATCK/ATCK2/CROUCH) at the end of Takeoff, but it writes vehicle.VehInput which GameWorld.cs:1104-1105 overwrites with fresh player input every tick BEFORE the next Think — so the clear is effectively a no-op on the live path. QC's per-tick button zero is faithful but the port's input-refresh architecture makes it inert (minor — input only reaches Frame, not Takeoff, so no fire/dive actually leaks).

[raptor.flight_controller | presentation] Free-flight avelocity controller (raptor_frame physics)  -  No visible model pitch/roll/yaw pose beyond entity angles; player glue origin+'0 0 32' done but no cockpit/exterior model (presentation only).

[raptor.cannon_aim_lock | presentation] Twin-cannon turret aim + target lock + lead predict  -  Aux lock crosshair color feedback (red==1 / green>0.5 / blue<0.5 by lock strength, UpdateAuxiliaryXhair) is client-only and not drawn.; locktarget==2 alternate direct-trace lock path unimplemented (default is 1, so off the live path).

[raptor.cannon_fire | presentation] Primary fire — twin laser cannon (1-1-2-2 cadence)  -  No EFFECT_RAPTOR_MUZZLEFLASH and no CSQCProjectile (PROJECTILE_RAPTORCANNON) visual for the cannon bolt (server hit logic only).; Spread sampling differs from Base: QC does normalize(dir + randomvec()*spread) (a cube offset added to forward, perturbing forward too); the port uses Prandom.Spread (uniform-in-disc projected onto right/up). Distribution shape differs — negligible at spread 0.01 but not bit-faithful.

[raptor.bombs | presentation] Secondary — cluster bombs burst into bomblets  -  No CSQCProjectile visual (PROJECTILE_RAPTORBOMB / RAPTORBOMBLET) for the falling bomb/bomblet (client-render trail only).; The burst FX is partial: RAPTOR_BOMB_SPREAD particle puff IS now emitted (Send_Effect equivalent), but the bomb-casing shell-fragment gibs (RaptorCBShellfragToss, the CSQC half of Damage_DamageInfo DEATH_VH_RAPT_FRAGMENT) are still client-only — see raptor.cb_shellfrag_gib.

[raptor.flares | presentation] Secondary — decoy flares (missile seduction)  -  No EF_FLAME flare glow / MDL_VEH_RAPTOR_FLARE model / scale 0.5 (decoy is invisible but functional).; Flare damage uses Health<=0 -> remove inline rather than the dedicated raptor_flare_damage event_damage hook; behaviorally equivalent (a shot flare with health 20 dies).; Seduction scan filters proj.VehGuideMode>=0; QC's raptor_flare_think scans g_projectiles by it.enemy==owner WITHOUT a guide-mode filter (the MIF_GUIDED_TRACKING gate is only on the incoming-alarm path, not the flare retarget). The port's extra filter could under-match guided rockets the flare should seduce.
[raptor.mode_switch | presentation] Secondary mode switch (bomb/flare select + cycle)  -  CSQCVehicleSetup (client HUD reticle update on mode change) not done (client concern).

[raptor.exit_land | presentation] Exit / auto-land (raptor_exit, raptor_land)  -  Land animation frame from altitude (hgt<128: (hgt/128)*25) + rotor spin tracked-but-invisible (no model).; antilag_clear on exit + CSQCVehicleSetup viewport restore are cross-boundary (net/client).

[raptor.impact | audio] Bounce impact damage / occupant shake (vr_impact, bouncepain)  -  CONFIRMED LIVE (fixup commit 4be8357, re-verified 2026-06-22): Raptor.Spawn no longer nulls vehicle.Touch, so the VehicleCommon.Touch dispatcher SpawnVehicle installs stands and calls vehic.VehicleDef.Impact on a hard touch (matches Racer.cs:217-219 / QC vr_spawn which does NOT reset .touch). Raptor.Think:231 snapshots vehicle.OldVelocity BEFORE the tick's physics (matches Racer.Think:296), and Spawn:141 seeds PlayTime = Time to clear the impact debounce on respawn — so vehicles_impact computes the true Velocity-OldVelocity delta. The raptor takes DEATH_FALL crash damage on hard impacts.; Dispatch chain VERIFIED: a BounceMissile-movetype raptor is integrated by MoveTypePhysics.cs:160 which calls PushEntity(doTouch:true) -> FlyMove.cs:107-110 invokes e.Touch for non-Solid.Not solids -> VehicleCommon.

[raptor.death | presentation] Death tumble + blowup (vr_death, raptor_diethink, raptor_blowup)  -  EXPLOSION_MEDIUM (death) + the per-think 5% EXPLOSION_SMALL ARE now emitted via EffectEmitter, but the findbetterlocation surface-nudge for the medium blast is not reproduced (FX at origin directly) — cosmetic offset only, matching every other vehicle vr_death port. No cosmetic wreck model / EF_NODRAW handling (presentation).

[raptor.painframe | presentation] Low-health pain frame (smoke + damage shake/roll jitter)  -  EFFECT_SMOKE_SMALL low-health smoke puff (origin + randomvec()*80) is not emitted — the headless Common layer has no particle/temp-entity emitter (noted VehicleCommon.cs PainFrame; presentation only).

[raptor.hud_crosshair | logic,values,timing,presentation] CSQC vehicle HUD + dual reticle / bomb dropmark crosshair  -  Entire CSQC HUD path absent: no Vehicles_drawHUD ammo bars, no per-mode reticle (vCROSS_BURST bomb / vCROSS_RAIN flare), no raptor_dropmark tracetoss bomb-impact predictor (gravity-bounce traced to a 2D drawpic marker, green while live / red after release), no aux lock crosshair (vCROSS_LOCK) color feedback. Client-only; the server logic these decorate (mode switch, lock strength) is all live elsewhere.

[raptor.cb_shellfrag_gib | logic,values,timing,presentation] CSQC cluster-bomb shell-fragment gib effect  -  The bomb-casing shell-fragment gibs tossed client-side when the cluster bomb bursts (RaptorCBShellfragToss spawns MOVETYPE_BOUNCE drawables with avelocity jitter that fade over ~2-3s) are not ported. Purely cosmetic debris; the bomb burst gameplay (raptor.bombs) is fully live.

[vehicle-framework.init.initialize | values,timing] vehicle_initialize — master gate, model/tag setup, placement, init hook  -  delayspawn + random jitter scheduling not ported (vehicles spawn at game_starttime only). STILL OPEN (sv_vehicles.qc:1278-1281). Keeps values/timing partial.; targetname controller / ACTIVE_NOT gating / vehicle_use not ported (map-scripted activation absent, sv_vehicles.qc:1178-1201/1276-1277). STILL OPEN. The targetname branch that ASSIGNS .team is unported, but the team-CLEAR branch (below) is independent of it.

[vehicle-framework.spawn.respawn | presentation] vehicles_spawn — reset to idle/ownerless/shootable at spawn point  -  EFFECT_TELEPORT spawn flash, CSQCMODEL_AUTOINIT, hook-removal sweep, and vehicles_reset_colors are deferred (client/presentation).

[vehicle-framework.think.tick | presentation] vehicles_think — per-tick think cadence + painframe + W2MODE mirror  -  The painframe smoke particle (EFFECT_SMOKE_SMALL) is deferred — note: EffectEmitter now EXISTS in the Common layer (used by the descriptor death/muzzle FX) so the old 'no headless emitter' rationale is stale, but PainFrame still does not emit the smoke. The authoritative jitter IS produced (see combat.painframe).

[vehicle-framework.enter.handshake | presentation] vehicles_enter — board guards, gunner branch, player freeze + link  -  MOVETYPE_NOCLIP + disableclientprediction=1 modeled as MoveType.None (player origin rewritten by the vehicle); behaviorally close but not the QC movetype.; SVC_SETVIEWPORT/SETVIEWANGLES + CSQCVehicleSetup, the temp_wepent weaponslot swap, CENTER_VEHICLE_ENTER notification, and RemoveGrapplingHooks are deferred (cross-boundary). The VehicleHud panel (the client view of CSQCVehicleSetup) IS fed live — see hud_camera_xhair. antilag_clear is now wired (pl.AntilagNeedsClear set on enter).

[vehicle-framework.enter.steal | presentation] g_vehicles_steal — enemy-team boarding (shield zero + intruder waypoint)  -  The CSQC presentation half is deferred: the WP_VehicleIntruder radar waypoint (g_vehicles_steal_show_waypoint) and the CENTER_VEHICLE_STEAL / _SELF centerprint notifications. The authoritative steal STATE (shield zero + flag backup/strip, restored on exit) is fully applied.

[vehicle-framework.exit.handshake | presentation] vehicles_exit — eject + restore player, return + recolor vehicle  -  SVC_SETVIEWPORT/SETVIEWANGLES + CSQCVehicleSetup, temp_wepent switchweapon restore, Kill_Notification(CPID_VEHICLES), and last_vehiclecheck are deferred. (The HUD exits cleanly: UpdateVehicleHud calls hud.Exit() once p.Vehicle is null.) antilag_clear is now wired (pl.AntilagNeedsClear set on exit).; The WP_Vehicle return waypoint (vehicles_showwp) itself is still CSQC-deferred (the return SCHEDULE is now set — see respawn.return_waypoint).

[vehicle-framework.combat.damage | presentation] vehicles_damage — per-weapon rate, shield-then-health, death eject  -  vehicle_shieldent hit flash (colormod/alpha angled to hitloc) is a CSQC visual — not drawn.; antilag_clear on death is deferred (cross-boundary).

[vehicle-framework.combat.painframe | presentation] vehicles_painframe — low-health smoke + DMGSHAKE/DMGROLL jitter  -  Smoke particle (EFFECT_SMOKE_SMALL at origin + randomvec()*80) is deferred — the only unported piece. NOTE: EffectEmitter now EXISTS in the Common layer (Wave 8 wired it for the descriptor death/muzzle FX), so the old 'no headless emitter' rationale is stale; PainFrame simply does not call it yet. The authoritative velocity/angle jitter IS produced.

[vehicle-framework.aim.locktarget | presentation] vehicles_locktarget — homing lock-on build/decay + lock sounds  -  The cockpit aux-crosshair lock-color update is LIVE for the building/locked target (UpdateVehicleHud -> SetAuxiliaryXhairLock projects VehLockTarget tinted by VehLockStrength); the per-target-class lock SEMANTICS are now also complete.

[vehicle-framework.projectile.shared | presentation] vehicles_projectile — generic vehicle bolt/rocket (splash, lifetime, shootable)  -  CSQCProjectile networking + the in-flight projectile trail/model visual are deferred (client/net). The muzzle EFFECT_* IS now emitted by the descriptors (Send_Effect via EffectEmitter).; g_bot_dodge list (bot AI) deferred.

[vehicle-framework.respawn.return_waypoint | presentation] vehicles_setreturn / showwp / return — return waypoint + living-vehicle return  -  No WP_Vehicle return waypoint / radar icon is shown while a vehicle returns (still deferred, CSQC). Base spawns a separate vehicle_return entity with vehicles_showwp as think; the port re-arms the vehicle's own think directly (functionally equivalent for the return SCHEDULE, but no radar icon).

[vehicle-framework.presentation.hud_camera_xhair | presentation] Vehicles_drawHUD / drawCrosshair / AuxiliaryXhair / CSQCVehicleSetup + cockpit camera  -  Cockpit/chase camera STILL dead: no client reads p.Vehicle to apply SVC_SETVIEWPORT (vehicle_viewport) — the pilot keeps the normal player view. (This is the only remaining DEAD piece of this row; it is its own viewport concern, not the HUD panel/crosshair.); Host-only feeder: UpdateVehicleHud reads LocalServerPlayer (host path). The cross-client VEHICLESTAT_* networking for a pure remote pilot is still unwired.; Vehicles_drawCrosshair is ported for the Raptor only (bomb/flare reticle + dropmark); the other vehicles' per-mode reticles are not yet wired (FeedRaptorReticle clears the centered reticle for non-Raptor).

[vehicle-framework.lifecycle.reset | logic] vehicles_reset — round/match-restart eject + clearreturn + respawn (.reset hook)  -  The QC `.reset = vehicles_reset` round-restart hook is NOT registered on any port vehicle (grep: no GtReset/.Reset assignment in src/.../Vehicles). On a round/match restart Base ejects any pilot (vehicles_exit VHEF_RELEASE), removes the return-helper entities (vehicles_clearreturn), and respawns the vehicle when active. The port has no equivalent reset path — a vehicle's state across a round boundary depends on whatever the generic spawn/respawn does, not this hook.

[vehicle-framework.death.gibs | logic,values,timing,presentation,audio] vehicle_tossgib / vehicles_gib_* — flying debris gibs on vehicle death  -  vehicle_tossgib (the vehicle_gib physics-entity subsystem) is UNPORTED — only a NOTE comment at Bumblebee.cs:502. A destroyed vehicle's vr_death does not spawn the flying debris gibs (model gib at a tag, tossed with velocity+avelocity, optional burn/explode, alpha fade, explosion FX + rocket-impact sound on contact). The descriptors emit a single death EXPLOSION_* FX (Wave 8) but no per-piece gibs.

[mutator-nades.toss.projectile | values] toss_nade: launch physics (size, newton-style, health, fuse)  -  Newton-style 0 is implemented as pure absolute _velocity rather than W_CalculateProjectileVelocity(e, e.velocity, _velocity, true); for non-default g_projectiles_newton_style this diverges from Base's velocity blending (style 0 is the shipped default, so identical at defaults).

[mutator-nades.boom.dispatch | presentation,audio] nade_boom: type dispatch + destroyed->normal fallback  -  The colored explosion particle (Send_Effect_Except with per-type EFFECT + player-color min/max via findbetterlocation) is omitted (render-only).; Base plays SND_Null on CH_SHOTS_SINGLE + SND_ROCKET_IMPACT on CH_SHOTS; the port plays only weapons/rocket_impact.wav.
[mutator-nades.boom.normal | presentation] Normal nade explosion (RadiusDamage)  -  DamageInfo (Damage_DamageInfo) network record omitted (render-only).

[mutator-nades.boom.napalm | presentation,audio] Napalm: fireballs + fountain fire damage  -  EFFECT_FIREBALL_LASER trail + EF_FLAME ball effect omitted (render). SND_NADE_NAPALM_FIRE spamsound on ball-eject is not emitted (audio partial).
[mutator-nades.boom.ice | presentation] Ice: freeze field  -  The muzzleflash/icefield particle gate is omitted (render).

[mutator-nades.boom.translocate | logic,presentation] Translocate: teleport thrower to detonation  -  move_out_of_solid is a minimal upward-nudge approximation (no builtin), so embedded-in-solid recovery is less robust than Base's full search.; MUTATOR_CALLHOOK(PortalTeleport) is not fired (no subscribers in port).
[mutator-nades.boom.spawn | presentation] Spawn: relocate respawn point  -  EF_STARDUST marker render + drawonlytoclient omitted (render).
[mutator-nades.boom.heal | presentation] Heal: healing orb (friend heal / foe harm / armor)  -  EFFECT_HEALING particle omitted (render).

[mutator-nades.boom.ammo | presentation] Ammo: ammo orb (refill friend / drain foe)  -  Per-weapon weapon_load[] persistent store collapsed onto each slot's live ClipLoad (port note). EFFECT_AMMO_REGEN particle omitted (render).

[mutator-nades.boom.entrap_veil_darkness | presentation,audio] Entrap / Veil / Darkness orbs + fields  -  Veil tint of enemies (render colormod) omitted; only the teammate alpha-hide is ported.; Darkness blind overlay (HUD_DarkBlinking full-screen fill) + SND_BLIND are CSQC-only and missing; only the NadeDarknessTime deadline is set server-side.

[mutator-nades.bonus.economy | logic,values] Bonus-nade economy (accrual / award / wipe)  -  GameRules_scoring_is_vip / flagcarrier multiplier: the VIP-kill medium bonus + flagcarrier time-score multiplier (g_nades_bonus_score_time_flagcarrier=2, scaled by KH key count) ARE now wired in OnPlayerDies / OnPlayerPreThink (GametypeEntities.ScoringIsVip + KeyHuntKeyCount). Whether those two helpers return Base-faithful values for every gametype (CTF/KA/TKA VIP, KH key carrier) is the residual values:partial risk — the per-second accrual path now reads CvarRaw(g_nades_bonus_score_time_flagcarrier) and the VIP/key branches, so the previously-MISSING flagcarrier cvar is now consumed.

[mutator-nades.damage.freezetag_revive | logic] Damage_Calculate: freezetag revive-nade  -  The port suppresses the self-nade damage+force but does NOT call freezetag_Unfreeze / set freezetag_revive_nade_health / send EFFECT_ICEORGLASS + INFO_FREEZETAG_REVIVED_NADE/CENTER_FREEZETAG_REVIVE_SELF notifications (documented cross-task seam — FreezeTag owns the unfreeze). So the revive-nade does not actually revive.
